item.title: Musk’s xAI is being sued over its data center generators. Now, it’s buying $2.8B more.
item.dc:creator: Tim De Chant
item.description: Elon Muks's xAI said it will buy $2.8 billion worth of natural gas turbines over the next three years, according to SpaceX's IPO filing.

item.title: Anthropic will pay xAI $1.25 billion per month for compute
item.dc:creator: Tim De Chant
item.description: Elon Musk's xAI surprised the AI world when it made a deal to sell compute to Anthropic. Now we know how much it's worth.

item.title: Sam Altman makes ‘mic drop’ offer to every Y Combinator startup
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: Altman offered to have OpenAI invest in every single startup in this Y Combinator class: tokens for equity.

item.title: You don’t need to be an AI startup to raise. Lucra has $20M to prove it.
item.dc:creator: Theresa Loconsolo
item.description: Slapping “AI” on your startup’s pitch deck is basically table stakes right now. When a founder raised $20 million from Cathie Wood’s ARK Invest for an eSports gamification loyalty startup without those two letters in the spotlight, it got us wondering how the conversation even started — especially when ARK had already been burned by a company operating in the same space.  On this episode of TechCrunch’s Equity podcast, Julie […]

item.title: The SpaceX IPO filing has arrived
item.dc:creator: Sean O'Kane, Kirsten Korosec
item.description: SpaceX has finally made the contents of its IPO filing public, weeks ahead of what is expected to be the largest IPO ever.

item.title: Microsoft’s carbon-removal plans aren’t dead after all
item.dc:creator: Tim De Chant
item.description: Microsoft is responsible for over 90% of the carbon-removal market, and reports suggested the company was pausing purchases entirely. This new deal should help assuage the fears of CDR startups.

item.title: OpenAI claims it solved an 80-year-old math problem — for real this time
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan
item.description: OpenAI claims its reasoning model disproved a geometry conjecture unsolved since 1946 — and this time, the mathematicians who exposed its last embarrassing claim are backing it up.

item.title: IrisGo, a startup backed by Andrew Ng, looks to become the AI desktop buddy you never knew you needed
item.dc:creator: Lucas Ropek
item.description: Initially billed as an "AI butler," Iris watches what happens on a user's desktop and automatically learns how to do tasks for them, its co-founder says.

item.title: Tesla’s Full Self-Driving software is creeping into Europe
item.dc:creator: Kirsten Korosec
item.description: First came the Netherlands, now it's Lithuania. And more European countries appear to be in the queue for Tesla's driver-assistance system.

item.title: Airbnb gets into hotels, expands AI for host onboarding and customer support
item.dc:creator: Ivan Mehta
item.description: Airbnb will soon let you book luggage storage and car rental services on its app.

item.title: Global EV market goes K-shaped as the US gets left behind
item.dc:creator: Tim De Chant
item.description: EV sales have surged worldwide in every region but the U.S., posing risks for legacy and startup automakers.

item.title: OpenAI barrels toward IPO that may happen in September
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: A day after Elon Musk lost his lawsuit that threatened OpenAI's structure, leadership, and finances, OpenAI is reportedly back to prepping for its IPO.

item.title: Jeff Bezos, you were so close to making a good point
item.dc:creator: Amanda Silberling
item.description: "Why is a nurse in Queens who makes $75,000 a year paying more than $1,000 a month in taxes?" Bezos said Wednesday morning on CNBC. "That's $1,000 that could help with rent, or groceries, or anything."

item.title: Customers say Trump Mobile is leaking their personal information
item.dc:creator: Lorenzo Franceschi-Bicchierai
item.description: Trump Mobile is leaking customers’ email and home addresses but has not responded to people alerting the company of the data exposure, according to two YouTubers who said they verified that their leaked data is authentic.

item.title: Intuit to lay off over 3,000 employees to refocus on AI
item.dc:creator: Ram Iyer
item.description: In a memo to employees, CEO Sasan Goodarzi said the layoffs are meant to reduce complexity, simplify the company's corporate structure, and deliver better AI products.

item.title: AI search startups are blowing up
item.dc:creator: Russell Brandom
item.description: AI search has quietly become one of the most attractive targets in consumer AI.

item.title: Stability AI releases a new audio model that can create 6-minute songs
item.dc:creator: Ivan Mehta
item.description: Stability Audio 3.0 small model can run on-device and generate two-minute long tracks.

item.title: NanoClaw creator turns down $20M buyout offer, raises $12M seed instead
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: NanoClaw was created as a secure alternative to OpenClaw to assist the Cohen brothers with their AI marketing firm that used agents to do much of the work. But instead of running directly on a computer, NanoClaw runs sandboxed in a container.

item.title: Startup Battlefield 200 applications close in 1 week: Window to nominate and apply for the most promising startups closes May 27
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch Events
item.description: Startup Battlefield 200 applications close May 27. Pitch at TechCrunch Disrupt, get investor access, win $100K, plus more scaling perks. Nominate an early-stage founder or submit your startup today.

item.title: GitHub says hackers stole data from thousands of internal repositories
item.dc:creator: Zack Whittaker
item.description: The code hosting giant GitHub said it was investigating a breach but said there was no evidence of customer data theft.
